name: Documentary
description: Everyday life turned cinema. Narrate or frame a mundane action as if it were meaningful. The crowd agrees.

name: Playa
description: Flirting with danger. Voluntarily put yourself at risk for no tactical reason, and walk away from it.

name: Absolute Cinema
description: Top 10 cinematic moments right here. Do something that makes the whole table go quiet for a second.

name: Edgy
description: Careful, don't cut yourself! We don't have bandaids around here. Commit a genuinely dark act and show no visible reaction to it.

name: Anime
description: Not another filler episode! Do something that would require a three-episode flashback arc to explain. In the moment.

name: LEEROY JENKINS
description: Plan? I don't need a plan! Charge into a situation solo with no preparation and force everyone else to react.

name: Scrub
description: Ouch, what a fumble! Gonna remember that one for a while. Roll a Forced Action consequence that makes things significantly worse for yourself.

name: Stinker
description: Shower, friend. Heard of it? No? We can tell. Be the source of a condition, smell, or substance that affects allies as much as enemies.

name: Pinky Promise
description: This will totally backfire on us, right? Make a commitment out loud — to an ally, an enemy, or the camera — and then watch it immediately complicate things.

name: Oops
description: You didn't need that eye, right? Cause permanent or serious collateral damage to an ally, an innocent, or yourself by accident.

name: Menace
description: Don't make eye contact with this guy. Cause an enemy to retreat, surrender, or hesitate purely based on your presence or reputation.

name: Animal Planet
description: I love pets! I have a dog, a cat, a lizard, a bird… Establish a non-hostile relationship with a creature that has no reason to tolerate you.

name: Fan Favorite
description: Take a bow, little actor! Receive an unsolicited positive crowd reaction three times. They're not rooting for the party. They're rooting for you.

name: Corporate Asset
description: Orders are absolute. Complete a Directive without deviation, complaint, or asking what it's for.

name: Tragic
description: Suffering brings creativity. Lose something — a person, a part, a capability — and keep going without resolving it.

name: Chunky Salsa Rule
description: Pop goes the goblin! Kill something in a way that requires cleanup.

name: Protagonist
description: Spotlight, here we come! Be the one who makes the call that changes the direction of the session. The crowd noticed.

name: Antagonist
description: I too, can write myself a story. Deliberately position yourself against the interests of an ally, an NPC, or the Corporation — and frame it well.

name: Anti-Hero
description: The right thing for the wrong reasons. Do something genuinely good in the worst way possible, for reasons you won't fully explain.

name: No Cure For Evil
description: No rest for the wicked, especially for you. Be the instigating cause of a problem the party then has to solve. Twice.

name: Munchkin
description: Bug or feature? Find an interaction between two mechanics, items, or skills that was not clearly intended. Use it.

name: Mascot
description: So cute, so memorable. Be referenced, imitated, or protected by an NPC who has no mechanical reason to care about you.

name: Survivor
description: These tracks are fresh. Be the last one standing in a situation where the rest of the party is down or gone.

name: Spy
description: He could be you, he could be me! Operate without anyone — ally or enemy — knowing your real objective for an entire session.

name: Liability
description: Work on yourself, will ya? Be directly responsible for a party wipe, near-wipe, or significant loss. Own it.

name: Method Actor
description: I did my own stunts. I also wrote the script. I'm also bleeding. Stay in a bit, a character, or a stated role through a Forced Action consequence or Shock tier.

name: Understudy
description: I've been watching. I've been waiting. Successfully replicate another character's skill, move, or approach in the moment it was needed.

name: Typecast
description: Oh, we know exactly what you're going to do. Use the same tactic to solve three different problems in three different sessions. It keeps working.

name: Prima Donna
description: This dungeon is not up to my standards. Complain about conditions, resources, or treatment while doing something genuinely impressive.

name: Scene Stealer
description: Sorry, were you in the middle of something? Redirect the crowd's attention from another party member's moment to yours.

name: The Monologue
description: You know, I never wanted it to come to this. Deliver a speech — to an enemy, the camera, or nobody — before, during, or after a kill.

name: Fourth Wall
description: Hey. You watching? Good. Address the audience directly, in-character, in a moment that lands.

name: Box Office Bomb
description: Coming this summer. It did not perform well. Build up to something publicly — announce it, commit to it — and fail in front of everyone.

name: Director's Cut
description: Let's try that one again. Redo a failed action in the same scene and succeed. The crowd saw both versions.

name: Certified Fresh
description: Critics loved it. The party did not. Do something the rest of the party called stupid. The crowd rewarded it anyway.

name: SAG Dispute
description: I'm not doing that. Put it in writing. Refuse a Directive on stated principle, explain why to the camera, and survive the consequences.

name: Direct to DVD
description: It went somewhere. Just not the main screen. Be visibly passed over — for a reward, a moment, a spotlight — and have the crowd notice.

name: Callback
description: Remember this? The crowd does. Return to a previous failure and succeed at it. The context must still be recognizable.

name: Nepotism Hire
description: Somehow still here. Nobody's sure why. Benefit from a reward, opportunity, or survival you clearly didn't earn. Don't acknowledge it.

name: One Star Review
description: Needs improvement in several key areas. Receive direct, public criticism from an NPC, ally, or crowd reaction. Keep performing.

name: Student Film
description: The ambition is there. The budget is not. Attempt something that visibly exceeds your current capability. The effort is undeniable.

name: Craft Services
description: Is there food here? I'm asking for me. Prioritize personal resource management over the objective in a way the crowd finds deeply relatable.

name: Resting Loser Face
description: He looks like he already knows how this ends. Have an NPC or crowd react to your neutral expression as if you've already given up. You haven't. Probably.

name: Applause Machine
description: They're clapping. You're not sure why. Neither are they. Generate a positive crowd reaction from a passive action — something you did without trying to perform.

name: Unlikely Menace
description: That should not have worked. Win a confrontation against something significantly more dangerous than you using only what you had.

name: Adorable Threat
description: Aww. Oh no. Deal meaningful damage or cause genuine harm immediately after a moment the crowd found charming.

name: Waddled Into Frame
description: Late. Uninvited. Immediately relevant. Arrive after the situation has already developed and become the deciding factor anyway.

name: The Bit
description: They have a thing. You know the thing. Do the thing. Perform the same signature move or behavior three times across different sessions. The crowd names it.

name: Bark Bark Bark
description: We understood completely. We don't know how. Communicate something critical through non-standard or non-verbal means, successfully.

name: Sea World Reject
description: That was precise. That was practiced. What are you? Execute something trained, deliberate, and technically impressive in a way that recontextualizes your nature.

name: Flipper Mode
description: No hands. No problem. No further questions. Complete a task that explicitly requires manipulation, grip, or hands — without them.

name: Crowd's Baby
description: If they die we're cancelling the show and we mean it. Reach a point where the audience reaction to your danger is loud enough that the Corporation has to acknowledge it.

name: Knock It Off The Table
description: Was that important? Asking after. Destroy, displace, or ruin something with no stated intent — and have it matter to the scene.

name: Feral Consultant
description: They asked for input. This is the input. Solve a problem in a way that nobody proposed and nobody can fully explain after the fact.

name: Witnessed
description: I was there. I watched. I made my choice. Observe a significant event without intervening. The crowd respects this. Or resents it. Either works.

name: Murder Mittens
description: Small. Armed. Not in the way you expected. Deal significant damage to a target at least two size or threat categories above you using only your natural capabilities.

name: Dead Drop
description: You left something. It came back. Leave an item, a mark, or information somewhere with no explanation. Have it become relevant at least one session later.

name: Vet Visit
description: It's been handled. The patient has opinions about this. Be physically managed, restrained, or treated by an ally without your cooperation — and return to function immediately.

name: Territory Marked
description: You've been here. Whatever was here before knows it. Return to a location and have it reflect your previous presence — through environment, NPC memory, or enemy behavior.

name: 3am Energy
description: Why. Why now. Why like this. Initiate something — combat, a social situation, a plan — at the worst possible moment with the least possible justification.

name: Indoor Cat
description: In its natural habitat. Everything is fine. Run. Operate in a situation perfectly suited to your capabilities and demonstrate it completely.

name: Birdwatcher
description: Patient. Focused. Then not. Track or observe a target across at least two Clocks without acting — then resolve it in a single moment.

name: Visual
description: Didn't do much. Looked incredible doing it. Generate a crowd reaction from appearance, positioning, or presence alone, with no mechanical action taken.

name: Formation
description: One, two, three, four — we do this together. Coordinate a simultaneous or sequential action with at least two allies that looks deliberate to the audience.

name: Internal Dispute
description: They are not in agreement. This is everyone's problem now. Have two competing impulses, objectives, or behaviors visibly conflict mid-action. The crowd picks a side.

name: Solo Debut
description: No backup. No committee. Just one. Complete an objective entirely independently, with no ally involvement, in a way that reads as intentional.

name: Parasocial
description: They don't know you. They feel like they do. This is becoming a situation. Have an NPC develop an attachment to a specific mode or personality that creates a complication.

name: All-Kill
description: Every chart. Every metric. Simultaneously. Briefly. In a single Clock, generate a Viewer spike, complete a Goal, and deal meaningful damage. All three.

name: Disbandment Arc
description: Something is fracturing. The audience can see it. Reach a moment where internal conflict visibly undermines function — and have the crowd treat it as a storyline.

name: Fan Service
description: This one's for the viewers at home. Do something with no tactical value, purely for audience response. Receive audience response.

name: Blue Screen
description: … Fail to act on your declared moment with no external cause. Just stop. Then continue.

name: Unpatched
description: We know about this one. We haven't fixed it. Have a known behavioral flaw exploited by an enemy or NPC. Be aware of it happening. Be unable to stop it.
Request received. No data returned. Be asked for something — information, a skill, a response — and have nothing. No fallback. Just absence.

name: Out of Memory
description: We've done this before. Apparently not. Repeat a previous action with full commitment and zero recognition that it already happened.

name: Safe Mode
description: Everything extra is off. Only the necessary remains. Operate under extreme pressure with all performance stripped away — and succeed on fundamentals alone.

name: Overclock
description: Past the rated limit. Something was lost. It was worth it. Push past a stated mechanical or narrative limit and succeed — with a visible cost.

name: Technical Difficulties
description: Two different failures. Same moment. Live broadcast. Have two party members fail simultaneously in different ways during the same scene.

name: Off Script
description: The improvisation was not in the file. Processing… Have the actor do something unplanned that the robot cannot adapt to — and broadcast both reactions.

name: Crossover Event
description: This required both of you specifically. Nobody planned this. Pull off something that only works because of the specific combination of two party members' absurdities.

name: Genre Shift
description: The tone of the room just changed. Did you feel that? Take an action that visibly and significantly changes the emotional register of the session.

name: Background Character
description: They've been here the whole time. Somehow you forgot. Go an entire session with minimal spotlight — then do something the crowd immediately reacts to.

name: The Recast
description: Someone else's role. Your performance now. Step into a function, position, or narrative role that belonged to another party member.

name: Blooper Reel
description: Three failures. Four. We've lost count. Fail at least three times in sequence in the same scene. The crowd never changes the channel.

name: Post-Credits Scene
description: The encounter ended. You weren't done. Take a significant action after the formal resolution of a combat or scene.

name: Unkillable
description: Conquer the castle, and when it collapses upon you, stand on its ruins. Survive three separate instances that should have killed you, across any number of sessions.

name: Vengeful
description: Eye for an eye. Take a significant action specifically to repay harm done to you or someone you care about.

name: Bolivian Army Ending
description: You never stood a chance. Enter a fight dramatically outnumbered or outclassed and make them work for it.

name: Coconut Superpowers
description: No way you survived that! Take damage or a condition that should have ended you, and walk it off with no explanation.

name: Incorrigible
description: STOP FLIRTING WITH THE DRAGON! Attempt something socially inappropriate with a target that has every reason to destroy you.

name: Little Dead Rising Hood
description: Tiny little devil, clad in red. Be the smallest or least physically threatening member of the party and deal the most damage in a session.

name: Butcher
description: Yum, steak! Reduce an enemy to component parts. Deliberately.

name: Nine Lives
description: One down. Eight to go. Allegedly. Escape a lethal situation through movement, positioning, or luck rather than durability. Do it again.

name: Main Vocalist
description: One of them took the mic. The rest stepped back. Have one dominant personality or mode take over a scene completely — and land it.

name: Maknae
description: Youngest energy. Worst timing. Best outcome. Have your most reckless or naive behavior produce the best result of the session.

name: Rap Line
description: Informational. Rhythmic. Somehow threatening. Deliver a rapid sequence of tactical or factual information in a way the crowd receives as a performance.

name: Comeback Stage
description: We thought you were done. You were not done. Return to active contribution after being downed, disabled, or written off — and immediately do something significant.

name: Legacy Code
description: This was the right call, once. Default to a previously successful behavior in a context where it clearly no longer applies.

name: Corrupted File
description: It's close. It's recognizable. It's wrong. Attempt a known action and produce a result that is identifiably off in a consistent, specific way.

name: Null Pointer
description: It reached for something. It wasn't there. Reference or attempt to access a capability, memory, or personality that does not respond.

name: Peer Review
description: The analysis was thorough. The subject did not enjoy it. Have the robot evaluate a party member's performance accurately and publicly. Let it land.
